info.name: Loyalty Points App
info.description:
JSON-RPC 2.0 API for the loyalty-points server. All methods are POSTed to the single /api endpoint. params MUST be a one-element array (the gorilla json2 codec unmarshals it into [1]interface{}).

Preset credentials live in collection variables — {{email}}, {{password}}, {{name}}, {{accountName}} — and are shared by Register and Login, so editing them in one place keeps both in sync and you can log in with whatever you just registered. Public methods (Register, Login) need no token. Register and Login capture the returned {{token}}; Register also captures {{userID}} and {{accountID}} so protected calls (e.g. the wallet methods) reuse them automatically.

Typical flow: run Register once, then Login (or any protected call) — variables are already populated.

Crediting is operator-only: Wallet.ProcessTransaction and Wallet.EarnPoints require an admin token (members cannot mint points into their own account). Wallet.SpendPoints is the only ledger write a member may call on an account they own. Wallet.ProcessTransactionBatch is admin-only: it applies an ordered batch sequentially in array order — a member token is rejected.

Import into Requestly via Import -> Postman.

request.description: Public. Creates a user and opens their first wallet account atomically, returning an access token so the caller is logged in immediately.

exec: console.log('captured token, userID and accountID from register');

request.description: Public. Authenticates with email and password and returns an access token. The test script stores result.token into the {{token}} collection variable.

item.name: Protected
item.description: These require a valid token and a role permitted to call the method (RBAC enforced by the authorization middleware). Run Login first to populate {{token}}.

request.description: Records a single transaction. ADMIN-ONLY (wallet:transact:all) — because it can credit (kind 'earn'), it is operator-only so a member cannot mint points; members use Wallet.SpendPoints instead. The owner is the account owner resolved from the account, not the acting admin. occurred_at is OPTIONAL — omit it (as here) and the server stamps it at processing time; supply an RFC 3339 value to back-date a transaction. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

Credits points. A thin convenience wrapper over ProcessTransaction with kind fixed to 'earn' — same idempotency, ownership, and audit guarantees. ADMIN-ONLY (wallet:transact:all): crediting is operator-only so a member cannot mint points into their own account — a member token is rejected. occurred_at is OPTIONAL (omitted here; the server stamps it). Re-send with the SAME ref to see idempotency: the balance stays put and the response comes back with "duplicate": true. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

Debits points. A thin convenience wrapper over ProcessTransaction with kind fixed to 'spend' — same idempotency, ownership, and audit guarantees. Callable by a member on an account they own; admins may act on any account. The overdraft floor is enforced: a spend that would drive the balance below zero is rejected and the balance is untouched. occurred_at is OPTIONAL. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

ADMIN-ONLY. Applies an ordered batch of transactions in a single request. The server processes them strictly in array order (so the overdraft floor sees them in chronology — the CLI sorts by occurred_at before sending). Each element is its own unit of work: a rejected element does not roll back earlier accepted ones, and a repeated ref is reported as a duplicate. Returns { result: { results: [...], summary: { accepted, duplicate, rejected } } }. per-row occurred_at is optional, same as the single method. A member token is rejected with 'forbidden: batch ingestion is admin-only'.

Opens an additional account for the calling user. The owner is NOT on the wire — it is always the authenticated caller, resolved from the token. name is OPTIONAL; the server defaults it when blank. Returns { result: { account_id, name, owner_id, balance, created_at } }. The test script stores the new account_id into {{newAccountID}} so you can drive transactions against it without clobbering the register-captured {{accountID}}. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

Reads a full account. Scoped to the caller: a member only sees accounts they own, and requesting another user's account id yields 'account not found' (indistinguishable from genuinely missing — no existence leak). Admins may read any account. Returns { result: { id, user_id, name, balance, created_at } }. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

Reads just the current balance. Same ownership scoping as GetAccountByID — members see only their own accounts, a non-owned id reads as 'account not found', admins may read any. Returns { result: { account_id, balance } }. Handy to call before/after an earn or spend to show the balance move. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

Renames an account. Callable by a member on an account they own (account:write:own) — the server takes the actor from the token and scopes the rename to that user, so renaming another user's account reads as 'account not found'. Admins (account:write:all) may rename any account. Returns the updated account: { result: { id, user_id, name, balance, created_at } }. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

ADMIN-ONLY (account:write:all). Applies a raw signed delta to the balance — positive to credit, negative to debit — bypassing the wallet's idempotency/audit unit of work; intended for operator corrections, not normal earns/spends. The overdraft floor is still enforced (a debit past zero returns 'insufficient balance'). A non-admin token is rejected with 'forbidden: balance adjustment is admin-only'. Returns { result: { account_id, balance } } with the balance after the delta. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

Returns every audit entry recorded for a transaction ref, oldest first — including accepted, duplicate, and rejected attempts. Scoped to the caller: a member (audit:read:own) sees only entries against accounts they own, so querying a ref recorded against another owner's account simply returns an empty trail (no existence leak); an admin (audit:read:all) sees every owner's. Returns { result: { transaction_ref, entries: [{ id, user_id, transaction_ref, account_id, owner_id, kind, points, outcome, reason, created_at }] } }. An empty entries slice is a valid result, not an error. Use the same ref you sent to Wallet.ProcessTransaction/EarnPoints/SpendPoints. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

Revokes the caller's outstanding access tokens by bumping their token_version (session epoch). The principal is taken from the token, never the wire — params is an empty object — so a caller can only log themselves out. Because the version is a single per-user value this is 'log out everywhere': every token issued before the bump is rejected by subsequent calls. Returns { result: { ok: true } }. After running this, the captured {{token}} is stale — re-run Login to get a fresh one. Sends Authorization: Bearer {{token}}.

exec: console.log('cleared {{token}} — it is now revoked; re-run Login');
